title: Trump Stokes Chaos in Congress as He Huddles With the G.O.P.
summary: Hours before visiting the Capitol, the president scrapped plans to sign a major housing bill, condemning “bad Republicans” for resisting his demands to ram through new voting restrictions.

title: Housing Package Passed by Congress Has Wide Appeal, but It’s No Quick Fix
summary: The first housing package in decades will take time to put into effect, testing the patience of families rankled by high prices.

title: Iran’s Loyalists Promote a Wider Nationalism, Unveiled Women Included
summary: Government supporters are showing off new ties with alleged former dissidents in a bid to show that they can withstand enemies at home as well as abroad.

title: After Bruising Primaries in New York, Democrats Look to November Elections
summary: The party secured key victories in New York City, but battles across the state suggest a larger fight awaits in the fall.

title: NY Jewish Leaders Wrestle With Israel Critics’ Sweep in Primary Elections
summary: Pro-Israel leaders in New York expressed alarm at the primary victories of three Democratic candidates backed by Mayor Zohran Mamdani. Progressive Jewish groups celebrated.

title: What Alex Bores’s New York Defeat Reveals About the Fight Over A.I. Money in Politics
summary: Alex Bores’s close loss in New York could pave the way for other Democrats to take political advantage of being attacked by the increasingly unpopular A.I. industry.

title: After New York’s Primary Elections, Democrats Face Five Big Questions
summary: The anti-establishment left surged. How shaken are party leaders?

title: Europe Heat Wave Latest News: After France’s Hottest Day Ever, U.K. Approaches June Record
summary: More than a dozen countries have issued urgent heat warnings. France saw its highest average temperature ever on Tuesday.

title: Why Is Europe the Fastest-Warming Continent
summary: The burning of fossil fuels is raising temperatures worldwide, but local factors, on land and at sea, determine which regions warm most rapidly.

title: New Clues Point to Taylor Swift Wedding Event at M.S.G.
summary: After weeks of speculation over Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce’s wedding plans, a permit has been filed for an event at Madison Square Garden over the July 4 weekend.

title: Gay Marriage Is Dividing Republicans, Again
summary: A look at the backlash to same-sex marriage — its strength, seriousness and the reasons behind it.

title: Support Builds on the Right for Prosecuting Women Who Get Abortions
summary: Sentiment is shifting amid frustration in the antiabortion movement that more abortions are happening now than when Roe v. Wade fell.

title: As Blockbusters Loom, Monkey Business at the Supreme Court
summary: An unlikely trio of justices issued a slashing critique of plea bargains that included several references to orangutans.

title: Pulte Trims Spy Agency, but Spares Bulk of Staff for Now
summary: Six intelligence officials were fired and nearly four dozen others were sent back to their home agencies elsewhere, former officials said.

title: With a Proposed $250 Bill, Trump Reimagines America by Putting Himself on Its Money
summary: Cash might be a relic, but — like royalty and emperors — President Trump still wants his face on American currency.

title: Judge Orders U.S. to Stop Seeking Medical Records of Transgender Youth
summary: The Justice Department had sought information about care provided at New York City hospitals, which were the subject of the ruling.

title: ICC Judges Sue Trump Administration Over Sanctions
summary: Kimberly Prost, a Canadian judge at the International Criminal Court, and two colleagues are suing the administration, saying the sanctions were a form of retaliation.

title: Dianna Russini Was an N.F.L. ‘Insider.’ Was She Also Out of Bounds?
summary: Ms. Russini’s closeness to a coach, Mike Vrabel of the New England Patriots, led to her breakup with The Athletic and its parent, The New York Times Company.

title: N.Y.P.D., F.B.I. Search Homes of Police Officials in Corruption Inquiries
summary: The searches, by police internal affairs investigators and federal authorities, stemmed from earlier scrutiny of Jeffrey Maddrey, the former chief of the department, according to people with knowledge of the matter.

title: Frank Carone, Longtime Eric Adams Associate, Is Arrested in Bribery Scheme
summary: Brooklyn federal prosecutors accuse Mr. Carone and his brother of profiting from the city’s migrant crisis. He was the chief of staff to former Mayor Eric Adams.

title: France Identifies Its First Case of Ebola
summary: A doctor who had traveled to the Democratic Republic of Congo was infected, the French health ministry said. The authorities said the risk to the wider population was low.

title: The Ebola Outbreak’s Central Mystery: Where Did This Virus Come From?
summary: Scientists believe that the Bundibugyo virus persists in an animal species, occasionally spilling over into humans. But they have yet to identify the species.

title: The Wearable Data Your Doctor Actually Wants
summary: Wearables can offer rough information about a person’s health, experts say, but only some metrics are medically useful.

title: A Billionaire’s Basquiat Collection Goes Up at Pérez Art Museum Miami
summary: Ken Griffin, the hedge fund manager, looks for artworks with a “wow” factor, never mind the number of zeros on the price tag. Ten of his Basquiats are on loan to the Pérez Art Museum Miami.

title: Thomas L. Friedman on the Clash at the Core of the Iran Deal
summary: How the world views of Jared Kushner and the founder of the Islamic Republic of Iran can help explain the issue at the heart of the negotiations.

title: President Narcissus and the Fetid Reflecting Pool
summary: Why the president’s latest renovation project is so irresistible and resonant.

title: Cuba Announces Sweeping Overhaul of Struggling Communist Economy, But Obstacles Remain
summary: The Cuban government announced a major economic restructuring, but experts say most changes would depend on buy-in from the Trump administration.
